programs.description: Ready… set… SAIL! In this bubbly, hands-on program, young inventors will build their very own soap-powered boat and watch it zoom across the water using the magic of science. Participants will explore how surface tension works as they design, build, and test their speedy little vessels. With plenty of time to experiment, decorate, and race, kids will discover what makes their boat go faster and farther. Perfect for curious kids who love to build, splash, and explore STEM in a fun way. All materials provided—just bring your creativity and get ready to make waves!

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion. As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Baseball
programs.description: Calling all baseball fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like throwing, hitting and fielding while developing your sense of teamwork, the rules of the game and sportsmanship. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in baseball, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program. Equipment: Campers must bring their own baseball glove.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Basketball
programs.description: Calling all basketball fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like dribbling, passing, layups and correct ball handling while demonstrating respectful play.  In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in basketball, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Crafts: Diamond Dots
programs.description: Create dazzling artwork, one dot at a time! Using tiny, sparkly "diamonds," you'll place each one onto a pre-printed adhesive canvas to build beautiful, shimmering designs. Diamond dotting is a fun and relaxing activity that encourages patience, focus, and creativity. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned crafter, you'll love watching your masterpiece come to life, dot by dot!

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Gold
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this third and final level of the Challenge Swim program you will learn relay starts while continuing to work on interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 500-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment, where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7 or Challenge Silver.

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Silver
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this second level of the Challenge Swim program, you will learn about relay dynamics, breaststroke and butterfly starts while continuing to work on flip and stroke turns, interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 400-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7 or Challenge Bronze.

programs.name: Club: Reading
programs.description: If you love books and reading, you will love this activity. Not only will you enjoy your favourite book, but you can share ideas and thoughts about your reading with others. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Computers
programs.description: Designed for first-time users or those with some knowledge, this introduction to computers focuses on how to use a device with confidence. Learn basic computer skills like navigation as well as how to create an email, access the web, create documents and more.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Computers: Smartphones and Tablets
programs.description: Want to get savvy with your smartphone or tablet device? Designed for users of various skill and competency levels, you will learn how to navigate and use your device, including texting and engaging on social media.Equipment: You must bring a smartphone or tablet.

programs.name: Computers: Smartphones and Tablets
programs.description: Want to get savvy with your smartphone or tablet device? Designed for users of various skill and competency levels, you will learn how to navigate and use your device, including texting and engaging on social media.Equipment: You must bring a smartphone or tablet.

programs.name: Computers: Video Game Design
programs.description: If you like playing video games, you will like the opportunity to learn how to design them. Combining technical elements and artistic design, instruction will focus on programming and game mechanics. Participants should have basic computer skills and a familiarity with video games.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Computers: Video Game Design
programs.description: If you like playing video games, you will like the opportunity to learn how to design them. Combining technical elements and artistic design, instruction will focus on programming and game mechanics. Participants should have basic computer skills and a familiarity with video games.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: From cookies to cakes, this activity is a sweet treat. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada's Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen. The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner - Small Group
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. With a focus on building confidence on the ice through fun games and activities, you will learn basic skating skills including balance and posture, forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Able to go on the ice alone and stand and walk on skates off the ice.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage and hockey or figure skates.
